resolution_summary: The exact-effect follow-up and registry-surveillance repairs are compatible for promotion despite sharing the senolytics coverage-assessment path. The exact-effect candidate reconciles abstract-located DKD, IPF, and AD-risk extraction progress and legacy IPF feasibility/tolerability records while preserving full-text/table debt. The registry-surveillance candidate only clarifies deferred fisetin frailty registry surveillance and preserves the ClinicalTrials.gov snapshot-ingestion blocker without adding extraction-grade registry records. Neither candidate supersedes the other.

evidence.summary: The candidate updates coverage and IPF-related records for abstract-backed exact-effect and feasibility/tolerability extraction while leaving full article/table extraction and D+Q bone supplement reconciliation as remaining debt.
The extraction-fidelity review accepts the abstract-backed IPF, DKD, AD-risk, and D+Q bone coverage boundaries and finds no correction required.
The safety-limitations review accepts the abstract-level safety boundary and preserves full-text/table and harmonized cross-human safety debt.
The synthesis-boundary review accepts the pilot efficacy limits and keeps remaining extraction debt visible.
The taxonomy-mapping review accepts the senolytics/cellular-senescence scope and endpoint-tier boundaries.
The candidate updates only the coverage assessment to name deferred fisetin registry candidates and preserves the source-snapshot requirement before adding any registry-status records.
The extraction-fidelity review accepts that no unsourced registry extraction was added and no endpoint values were introduced.
The synthesis-boundary review accepts that the deferred registry candidates remain a surveillance gap until source snapshots are fetched.
The taxonomy-mapping review accepts the senolytics registry-surveillance scope and keeps registry status separate from clinical effect claims.
